DISCOVERY 301, PLAINTIFF JULIA PADILLA'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITION AND FOR SANCTIONS. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF JULIA PADILLA'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITION OF DEFENDANT AMERICAN HONDA'S PMQ IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHALL PRODUCE THE PMQ FOR DEPOSITION BY JANUARY 30, 2026. DEFENDANT AND ITS COUNSEL (LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SMITH LLP) ARE JOINTLY AND SEVERALLY LIABLE FOR REASONABLE MONETARY SANCTIONS OF $3,035 THAT SHALL BE PAID BY JANUARY 30, 2026. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

Hearing held. ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument modifies its tentative ruling follows:

PLAINTIFF JULIA PADILLA'S Motion To Enforce Court Order Compelling Pmq Deposition; Request For Additional Monetary Sanctions. Court orders the PMQ Deposition must take place on March 24, 2026. Request for monetary sanctions are denied.

Plaintiffs' counsel is to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/HEK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-29-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF JULIA PADILLA'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITION AND FOR SANCTIONS.

Appearances: Alexander Khoubian of Alpha Law Firm, PC (310.400.5652) for plaintiff and moving party; No other appearances.

This matter is not argued. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff Julia Padilla's motion to compel deposition of defendant American Honda's PMQ is granted. There is good cause for the PMQ deposition as it is reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (CCP 2017.010.) Plaintiff demonstrates reasonable meet and confer efforts and defendant has not been forthcoming with the discovery process. (Khoubian Decl., par. 8 and Ex. G.) Defendant does not cogently explain how the discovery is overly broad, irrelevant, or unduly burdensome. Defendant did not act with substantial justification.

Defendant shall produce the PMQ for deposition by January 30, 2026. Defendant and its counsel (Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP) are jointly and severally liable for reasonable monetary sanctions of $3,035 that shall be paid by January 30, 2026. Order signed. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-23-2025 Continued To Apr-13-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Motion To Compel Deposition And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-28-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JULIA PADILLA'S Motion To Compel Deposition And For Sanctions.

Plaintiff's motion to compel deposition of defendant American Honda Motor Co., Inc.'s PMQ, with production of documents, is denied. Plaintiff filed and served her motion 15 court days before hearing. CCP 1008 requires 16 court days, with an additional two court days where service is made electronically. The court further notes that Plaintiff's meet and confer efforts about the PMQ topics and her document requests were inadequate. Plaintiff attempted to informally resolve her dispute about the date but did not attempt to informally resolve disputes about the topics and document requests. Moreover, Plaintiff's moving papers fail to "set forth specific facts showing good cause justifying the production for inspection of any document . . . described in the deposition notice." (CCP 2025.450(b)(1).)

Proposed order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
